document.source_title: World News in Brief: More attacks in Ukraine, violence against children in Haiti, refugee IDs in Africa
document.summary: Recent attacks in Ukraine have resulted in the deaths of at least 11 civilians and injuries to nearly 200 others, including children. Humanitarian efforts are being hampered as aid workers face increasing dangers. In Haiti, children are suffering from escalating gang violence, with a significant rise in recruitment and violence against minors. Meanwhile, the UN is advocating for the inclusion of refugees in national digital ID systems across Africa to enhance their access to services.
key_takeaways: 11 civilians killed and nearly 200 injured in Ukraine attacks.
Humanitarian workers are increasingly targeted during aid delivery.
Gang violence in Haiti has led to a tripling of child recruitment by gangs.
Children make up 30-50% of gang members in Haiti.
UN calls for digital ID inclusion for refugees in Africa.
4.4 million people globally are reported stateless or of undetermined nationality.
document.why_it_matters: The ongoing violence in Ukraine and Haiti highlights the urgent need for humanitarian support and protection for vulnerable populations, particularly children. The push for digital ID inclusion aims to improve the lives of refugees and stateless individuals, ensuring they have access to essential services and reducing their dependence on humanitarian aid.

claims.claim_text: At least 11 civilians were killed in Ukraine attacks.
claims.evidence_text: Over the past three days, frontline attacks killed at least 11 civilians and injured nearly 200 others.

claims.claim_text: Children are increasingly targeted by gangs in Haiti.
claims.evidence_text: UN officials warned that the recruitment and use of children by gangs nearly tripled in 2025 compared to the previous year.

claims.claim_text: UN is advocating for digital ID inclusion for refugees.
claims.evidence_text: UN representatives met on Monday in Côte d'Ivoire to advance the inclusion of refugees and stateless persons in national digital identification systems across Africa.

entities.name: Haiti
entities.description: Country facing escalating gang violence affecting children.
entities.evidence_text: Children in Haiti are facing escalating violence as armed gangs increasingly recruit minors.

entities.name: UNHCR
entities.description: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees, advocating for refugee rights.
entities.evidence_text: Inclusion in national digital foundational ID systems is central to UNHCR's broader agenda.

events.event: Drone attack on Ukrainian NGO vehicle
events.evidence_text: On May 15, a marked vehicle of a Ukrainian NGO came under direct drone attack while delivering hot meals.
In a separate incident on May 14, a drone struck an armoured vehicle operated by a local partner of World Central Kitchen.

events.event: Highest civilian casualties recorded in Kherson City
events.evidence_text: According to the UN’s Human Rights Monitoring Mission to Ukraine, in April, Kherson City recorded the highest number of civilian casualties.
